title: bug: copy fails if registry does not yet exist and blob checking is done
body:
I have a weird error which is potentially a bug in skopeo, where I copy to a not yet existing repository:

```shell
"skopeo" "--insecure-policy" "copy" \
"docker-archive:///persist/repos/custodian/components/contract-manager/.output/package/contract-manager-service" \ 
"docker://registry.gitlab.com/data-custodian/custodian/nix/temporary/contract-manager-service:0.0.1-e13a1cadd785"
```

```
Getting image source signatures
FATA[0002] trying to reuse blob sha256:3dc63f619455dc97812a554641f188908c4c9a8303f3f5f0e46485b080090043 at destination: checking whether a 
blob sha256:3dc63f619455dc97812a554641f188908c4c9a8303f3f5f0e46485b080090043 exists in 
registry.gitlab.com/data-custodian/custodian/nix/temporary/contract-manager-service: requested access to the resource is denie
```

- **Note: the repository: docker://registry.gitlab.com/data-custodian/custodian/nix/temporary does not yet exist**.
- The repo `docker://registry.gitlab.com/data-custodian/custodian/nix` however exists and a copy to it succeeds.

I have seen skopeo normally work and create the repository but, on 1.17 it fails?

comments.body: fyi: all good I guess its due to that subfolders are not allowed to be repos again.

Thanks for reaching out.

For more context, “checking whether a blob … exists” is typically the first access that requires any kind of permission while uploading, so if the registry is refusing the operation, that’s typically how that is reported.

Auto-creating repositories would be a registry-side feature Skopeo can do nothing about; there is nothing in the vendor-neutral registry protocol that would allow Skopeo to create a repository.
